grammar_notes: Uses basic N5 patterns: particle に (indirect object), の (nominalizer), は (topic marker), い-adjective predicate. The nominalized verb 会うの is acceptable N5 grammar
target_vocab_notes: 難しい appears clearly as the predicate adjective in the sentence
kana_notes: かのじょにあうのはむずかしい。 matches perfectly with 彼女に会うのは難しい。
overall_notes: Clean N5 example with proper nominalization pattern. Good demonstration of 難しい in context

grammar_notes: Uses N5 comparison pattern: X は Y よりも Adj. All grammar elements are basic N5 (は, より, も, い-adjective)
target_vocab_notes: 村 appears clearly in the sentence comparing towns and villages
kana_notes: Kana reading is accurate: まちはむらよりもおおきい。
overall_notes: Excellent example of basic comparative structure with clear use of target vocabulary

grammar_notes: Uses basic N5 patterns: は (topic), で (location), past tense ました form. ちいさな is the attributive form of 小さい which is acceptable N5
target_vocab_notes: 村 appears in the locative phrase ちいさな村で
overall_notes: Clear, natural N5 sentence with proper use of target vocabulary in context

grammar_notes: Uses basic N5 patterns: に (location), が (subject marker), ある (existence verb), counter 本. All elements are N5
target_vocab_notes: 鉛筆 appears with counter 五本の
overall_notes: Good example showing pencil with appropriate counter 本. Clear N5 grammar

grammar_notes: Uses N5 patterns: は (topic), の (possessive/descriptive), を (object), dictionary form verb. The phrase 先の細い uses の to connect modifying adjective 細い to 先
target_vocab_notes: 鉛筆 appears as direct object in 鉛筆を使う
overall_notes: Slightly more complex sentence structure but all grammar is N5. Good demonstration of target vocab with descriptive modifiers

grammar_notes: Uses basic N5: に (indirect object), を (object), た (past tense). All patterns are fundamental N5
target_vocab_notes: 長い appears as adjective modifying 手紙
overall_notes: Clean, straightforward N5 example. Good use of 長い as adjective modifier

grammar_notes: Uses N5 pattern: X は Y が Adj (topic-が-adjective pattern). This is a standard N5 structure for describing attributes
target_vocab_notes: 長い appears in 足が長い (long legs)
overall_notes: Excellent basic N5 example using standard は...が pattern. Natural expression

grammar_notes: Uses basic N5: は (topic marker), だ (copula). The structure X は Y だ is fundamental N5
target_vocab_notes: 長い appears in 長い足 (long feet/legs)
overall_notes: Very basic N5 sentence. Clear demonstration of target vocabulary

grammar_notes: Uses N5 patterns: は (topic), で (location of action), ました (past polite). All basic N5 elements
target_vocab_notes: 生まれました appears in past polite form in 村で生まれました
overall_notes: Clean N5 sentence. Natural use of 生まれる with location marker で

grammar_notes: Uses N5: が (subject), どこで (where), か (question marker in embedded clause), 知っている (て-form + いる for knowing). While this uses ている, 知っている is one of the most fundamental ている expressions taught at N5 level (state of knowledge)
target_vocab_notes: 生まれた appears in embedded question clause どこで生まれたか
overall_notes: 知っている is acceptable as it's a fundamental state verb. Embedded question structure is within N5 range. Target vocab clearly present

grammar_notes: Uses N5: に (indirect object/beneficiary), time expression 先週, が (subject), た (past tense). All basic N5 elements
target_vocab_notes: 生まれた appears in 子供が生まれた (a child was born)
overall_notes: Natural N5 sentence. Clear use of 生まれる in context of childbirth

grammar_notes: Uses basic N5: は (topic), いつも (always - N5 adverb), を (object), 読む (dictionary form). All elements are fundamental N5
target_vocab_notes: 雑誌 appears as object in 雑誌を読む
overall_notes: Simple, clear N5 sentence. Perfect for demonstrating 雑誌 in basic context

grammar_notes: Uses basic N5: おや (interjection), は (topic), です (copula), ね (sentence-ending particle). All fundamental N5 elements
target_vocab_notes: 今朝 appears clearly as topic with は
kana_notes: Kana matches perfectly: おや、けさははやいですね。
overall_notes: Natural conversational N5 sentence. Good example of 今朝 in context

grammar_notes: Uses N5: は (topic), time expression, つき (attached - from つく), を (object), た (past tense). The expression バターつき means 'with butter' using つき which is acceptable N5
target_vocab_notes: 今朝 (けさ) appears as time expression
overall_notes: Clear N5 sentence. Natural use of 今朝 with past tense action

grammar_notes: Uses basic N5: は (topic), とても (very - N5 adverb), かった (past tense of い-adjective). All fundamental N5
target_vocab_notes: 今朝 (けさ) appears as topic
overall_notes: Excellent basic N5 example. Simple and clear use of 今朝

grammar_notes: Uses N5: の (possessive), は (topic), でしょう (polite presumptive). While でしょう can be considered slightly advanced, it's generally taught at N5 for weather predictions and is less assertive than だろう
target_vocab_notes: 晴れ appears in 晴れ時々雨 (sunny with occasional rain)
overall_notes: でしょう in weather contexts is standard N5 usage. Target vocab clear in weather forecast context

grammar_notes: Uses N5: ない (negative form), でしょう (presumptive). The negative + でしょう pattern is acceptable N5 for predictions
target_vocab_notes: 晴れ appears in negative form 晴れない
overall_notes: Simple N5 sentence. Clear negative form of target vocabulary with polite presumptive

grammar_notes: Uses basic N5: は (topic), time expression 昨日, を (object), た (past tense). All fundamental elements
target_vocab_notes: 夕飯 appears as object in 夕飯を作った
overall_notes: Clean, straightforward N5 sentence. Natural use of 夕飯

grammar_notes: Uses N5: は (topic), もう (already - N5 adverb), ました (past polite), か (question). All basic N5
target_vocab_notes: 夕飯 appears as topic
overall_notes: Perfect basic N5 question. Natural and common usage

grammar_notes: Uses basic N5: の (possessive), です (copula), よ (emphasis particle). All fundamental N5
target_vocab_notes: 夕飯 appears with の possessive in 夕飯の時間
overall_notes: Excellent simple N5 example. Common household expression

grammar_notes: Uses N5: に (particle), 食べに (purpose 'to eat'), 行く, ましょう (suggestion). While ましょう is volitional, basic suggestions with ましょう are taught at N5 level
target_vocab_notes: 一緒 appears with に particle meaning 'together'
overall_notes: ましょう for basic suggestions is acceptable N5. Natural use of 一緒に

grammar_notes: Uses N5: に (direction/purpose), ませんか (negative question for invitation). This is standard N5 invitation pattern
target_vocab_notes: 一緒 appears with に meaning 'together'
overall_notes: Standard N5 invitation structure. Clear use of target vocabulary

grammar_notes: Uses N5: と (with), に (direction), 行こう (volitional), よ (particle). While 行こう is volitional form, when used with よ for casual suggestions it's taught at N5
target_vocab_notes: 一緒 appears with に in と一緒に (together with)
overall_notes: Casual volitional with よ is acceptable N5 for suggestions. Natural colloquial expression

grammar_notes: Uses N5: は (topic), ぐらい (about/approximately - N5), ます (polite present). All basic N5 elements
target_vocab_notes: どれ appears in どれぐらい (how much)
overall_notes: Good N5 example. どれぐらい is standard N5 quantity expression

grammar_notes: Uses basic N5: が (subject marker), の (possessive), です (copula), か (question). All fundamental N5
target_vocab_notes: どれ appears as subject asking 'which one'
overall_notes: Perfect basic N5 which-question. Clear use of どれ

grammar_notes: Uses N5: に (direction), て (te-form conjunction), どれぐらい (how long). The construction Vて、どれぐらいたつ asking 'how long has it been since...' is acceptable N5
target_vocab_notes: たつ appears but means 'to pass (time)' not 'to stand' - different meaning of same verb
overall_notes: While grammar is N5-appropriate, note that たつ here means 'pass (time)' not 'stand'. Different usage of same verb. Acceptable if target vocab covers both meanings

grammar_notes: Uses N5: は (topic), なぜか (for some reason - N5), の方が (comparison pattern - N5), だ (copula). All elements are N5
target_vocab_notes: 元気 appears in 元気だ (energetic/lively)
overall_notes: Good N5 example using comparative structure. Natural use of 元気

grammar_notes: Uses basic N5: さん (honorific), は (topic), informal question with rising intonation. Very basic N5 pattern
target_vocab_notes: 元気 appears in casual question 元気？
overall_notes: Perfect simple N5 example. Common greeting expression

grammar_notes: Uses N5: の (possessive), は (topic), どう (how), か (question particle), しら (sentence-ending particle). While かしら is somewhat feminine/dated, it's introduced at N5 level
target_vocab_notes: 天気 appears with honorific お as お天気
overall_notes: Acceptable N5 with かしら sentence-ending particle. Natural weather question

grammar_notes: Uses N5: この (this), い-adjective 悪い, は (topic), もう (already/anymore), たくさん (enough - can mean 'had enough' in this context), だ (copula). All N5 elements
target_vocab_notes: 天気 appears in この悪い天気 (this bad weather)
overall_notes: Good N5 example. もうたくさんだ is a common N5 expression meaning 'had enough'

grammar_notes: Uses N5: は (topic), に (goal marker), なる (become), でしょう (presumptive), ね (particle). While でしょう is presumptive, it's standard N5 for predictions and less advanced than だろう

grammar_notes: Uses N5: は (topic), を (object), の (nominalizer), が (subject), である (formal copula - N5), と (quotation), わかった (past tense of わかる). While である is formal, it's introduced at N5. The nominalized clause is acceptable N5
